# Codex diff review — I-arch-011 FIX-C (PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY) — iter 2 (your iter-1 P1 + P2 addressed)

## WHAT CHANGED SINCE YOUR ITER-1 REVIEW
Your iter-1 verdict was REQUEST_CHANGES: ONE P1 (parallel-verify cost not reconciled to the parent budget
gate) + two P2. BOTH the P1 and the actionable P2 are now fixed. The diff is now 2 files (was 1).

**P1 ADDRESSED (cost reconciliation) — `provenance_generator.py` parallel-verify branch (~2789-2818):**
You were CORRECT — verified against source: `_RUN_COST_CTX` is a ContextVar (openrouter_client.py:99);
`_add_run_cost` does `_RUN_COST_CTX.set(...)` (:303); each worker runs in `_parent_ctx.copy().run(...)`, so
the judge's `_add_run_cost`/`check_run_budget` mutate the COPY and are lost to the parent → the parallel
verify spend bypassed PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN. FIX = the EXACT pattern the codebase already documents for the
credibility offload (`ledger_cumulative` docstring, openrouter_client.py:345-351): the cost LEDGER is a
process-global lock-protected per-session accumulator (`append_cost_ledger_row` bumps it from any thread,
NOT a contextvar), so we snapshot `ledger_cumulative(run_id)` BEFORE the pool, run the pool, then
`_add_run_cost(after - before)` + `check_run_budget(0)` on the PARENT context. The gate is now inclusive of
the parallel judge spend; a cap breach raises BudgetExceededError (same terminal behavior as the serial
per-call check). Cost-accounting only — verdicts unchanged.

**P2-1 ADDRESSED (force-exact) — `run_gate_b.py`:** added `"PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY"` to
`_BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS` so the slate value 16 is forced EXACTLY (a stray env value can neither exceed
the 16-worker cap nor silently revert to serial=1).

**P2-2 (telemetry shared-dict increments) — ACCEPTED, not changed:** it is PRE-EXISTING in the fix#19
parallel path (not introduced by this diff), you confirmed it does NOT change kept/dropped verdicts, and the
GIL makes the int increments effectively atomic. Captured as a cosmetic follow-up, not fixed in this diff.

## BEHAVIORAL PROOF (unchanged, banked)
`scripts/iarch011_parallel_verify_gate.py` (real glm-5.1 judge + PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=16 on the banked drb_78
corpus): the enrichment verify COMPLETES in 17.4 min (was ~173 min serial) and keeps 1746 cited / 657
distinct sources on the REAL enforce path. Real-judge keep-rate 39/40=97%.

.\AGENTS.md:17:   - The side judges (entailment / semantic_conflict / credibility) are NOT one of the 4 locked roles;
.\architecture.md:3:> **ARCHITECTURE DNA (operator-locked 2026-06-13, CLAUDE.md §-1.3):** The pipeline is **WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, not FILTER-AND-CAP.** (1) **Weight** every relevant source by credibility — don't filter it out (social media stays at low weight; the tier classifier IS the weighting system). (2) **Consolidate** same-claim sources into a basket and keep them ALL as multi-citation corroboration. (3) Decide **faithfulness against the whole basket**, never one span (single-source verification is a blind spot). The faithfulness engine (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; everything else is a weight or a consolidation. **Banned:** hardcoded caps/targets/thinners to force a breadth number. **Surgical, not rewrite.** See CLAUDE.md §-1.3, `docs/polaris_pipeline_canonical.md`, `docs/pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md`, I-arch-001 (#1245).

.\CLAUDE.md:25:5. **Both Claude and Codex run independent line-by-line audits in parallel.** Cross-review combines findings.
.\CLAUDE.md:59:1. **WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER.** Every relevant source flows through to composition carrying a credibility **weight** (peer-review status, journal/venue reputation, gov / institute / news / social media). Do NOT hard-drop a source to hit a number. Social media STAYS at low weight — sometimes it reports a real journal. The tier classifier (T1–T7) and `authority_score` ARE the weighting system: use them as a per-citation **weight surfaced to the user**, never as a rank-then-drop hard filter.

.\CLAUDE.md:676:1. **One Codex `exec` at a time.** NEVER run two `codex exec` invocations in parallel. Each Codex call spawns sub-processes (~3 alive concurrently) that linger after exit; multiple in flight = OS-level resource exhaustion. Run codex in foreground with a 9-min timeout, OR if backgrounded, await completion before queuing the next.
.\CLAUDE.md:680:3. **No parallel pytest / npm / playwright runs.** Single test run at a time per workspace. If a previous run is still active (`Get-Process -Name python,node`), wait for it OR kill it before starting a new one.

.\docs\credibility_adaptive_weighting_and_bothsides_recs_2026_06_08.md:1:# POLARIS Source-Credibility & Multi-Perspective Recommendations Report\n### Driving two revisions to `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md` — LAYER 1 (scoring) and LAYER 6 (composition + UI)\n\n**Author:** Lead analyst (Claude). **Date:** 2026-06-08.\n**Inputs:** three research streams (per-question adaptive credibility weighting; neutral multi-perspective presentation; friendly UI/artifact patterns).\n**Status:** revision driver. Each layer below states what it **REPLACES** in the Codex-APPROVE'd 2026-06-07 plan, then the concrete mechanism, auditability, reliability, and POLARIS wiring.\n\n**Invariants preserved across both revisions** (these are not negotiable and the revisions do not weaken them): the LLM credibility-judge is **advisory only** — `strict_verify`'s six per-sentence checks (`provenance_generator.py:1156`) remain the binding faithfulness gate; every new layer is **flag-gated, default-OFF byte-identical**, fail-loud on missing inputs; weight is **evidence quality/tier, never headcount**; every displayed aggregate is **one click from the cited `[#ev:id:start-end]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict**; the credibility prior stays **self-computed and verifier-overridable** (no single external rater hardwired); independence-collapse (`synthesis/independence_collapse.py`) still runs before any weighted tally; the contested-topic adjudication **reuses the existing 4-role / voter-arbiter pipeline** (`roles/role_pipeline.py` + `honest_sweep_integration.py`), open-weight voters only — no parallel path is built.\n\n---\n\n## 1. LAYER 1 (scoring): adaptive per-question credibility-skill, replacing the fixed domain-rubric selector\n\n### 1.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 2a/2b (L1)** selects one of **three fixed reliability rubrics** (clinical → RCT+peer-review+RoB; econ/policy → design-validity; qualitative → CERQual) keyed off the detected domain. That is still a small lookup table: domain → rubric. The operator constraint is explicit — **replace fixed domain rubrics** with a **generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill the LLM applies by reasoning per question**. Research stream #1's central finding is that the frontier (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) has already moved this way, and that Perplexity's baked-in EEAT/domain-authority scoring is the closest-to-forbidden counter-example (gameable, popularity-biased, ~60% Google-overlap). We copy the reasoned-generic camp.\n\nThe revision: **domain stops being a selector and becomes context that primes one generic skill.** There is exactly one credibility skill; the detected `scope_template` domain is passed in as a hint the skill reasons over ("this is a clinical efficacy question, so weight trial design and RoB heavily"), not a branch that swaps rubrics. This scales to 10,000+ fields with zero per-field config.\n\n### 1.2 The exact mechanism — credibility-skill / guardrail prompt × (reliability × relevance)\n\n**Step A — deterministic signals (POLARIS produces these; unchanged substrate).** `authority_model.score_source_authority` and `tier_classifier` already emit, per source, the field-agnostic primitives: `authority_score [0,1]`, `source_class`, `corroboration_count` (independent-host agreement), `authority_confidence`, `signal_scores` (the A–E sub-scores), plus `junk_class` / `predatory_oa` (`source_class.py:71-89`). Independence-collapse (`independence_collapse.py`) emits `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + copy flag. **None of this is replaced.** These are the inspectable, host-name-free, self-computed inputs (LAW VI: zero host literals in code).\n\n**Step B — the credibility skill consumes those signals and weights them per question.** A new pure prompt module, `authority/credibility_skill.py`, runs a **JudgeRank-style 3-step decomposition** (arXiv 2411.00142) — chosen because each step is inspectable text and is itself the audit template:\n\n1. **Query analysis** — identify what THIS question actually needs (the claim type, what would count as strong evidence for it).\n2. **Source analysis** — for each candidate source, a query-AWARE assessment applying **generic SIFT + lateral-reading + provenance heuristics**, NOT a domain table. The skill's instruction block encodes the domain-agnostic primacy heuristics that are OpenAI's own deep-research pattern: *prefer the primary/official source over an aggregator or SEO blog; prefer the original paper over a survey/secondary summary; corroborate laterally (does independent coverage agree); weigh source incentives/track record; downgrade stale-but-authoritative and retracted/superseded* (the latter wires to the existing `supersession.py` Phase 3 signal).\n3. **Two-axis judgment with rationale** — emit, per source, **RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE** (the EFSA/OHAT weight-of-evidence two-axis, which POLARIS currently conflates into one tier): reliability = internal validity/method given what the skill read in the signals; relevance = directness to this specific question. The product is the per-question credibility weight. **Crucially, the skill is told its priors are the `authority_model` signals and that it may up- or down-weight them with a stated reason** — it does not invent authority from nothing, and it cannot silently override `authority_confidence=LOW` into a fabricated HIGH (thin OpenAlex/ROR signal stays honestly LOW).\n\n**Output schema (machine-parseable, bound to the schema discipline already in §8.3.9):** per source — `{reliability_score, relevance_score, credibility_weight, rationale_string, signals_cited:[...]}`. The weight is **a visible, revisable rationale string, never a hidden scalar and never a fine-tuned-in behavior** (this is why we reject the CAG fine-tune approach from stream #1: opaque learned weights are exactly the auditability failure POLARIS differentiates against — borrow CAG's *evidence* that credibility-conditioning gives noise robustness, and its three generic dimensions of relevance/timeliness/reliability, but implement them at inference time as a prompt-visible signal).\n\n### 1.3 How it stays AUDITABLE (auditable by construction, not bolted on)\n\nThe judgment IS a reasoning trace, so it is emitted verbatim and **bound to the same provenance substrate POLARIS already has**:\n\n- The per-source `credibility_weight` + `rationale_string` + `signals_cited` attach to the source's `AuthorityResult` as **additive fields** (mirroring how `signal_scores`, `junk_class`, `predatory_oa` are already additive side-outputs on the dataclass), emitted on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n- At claim time, the weight flows into the four Phase-1/Phase-8 disclosure fields on `SentenceVerification` (`credibility_weight`, `independent_origin_count`, `certainty_label`, `span_verdict`), so each verified sentence shows **which source was weighted how, and why**, one click from its `[#ev]` span.\n- Because the skill cites the deterministic `signals_cited` it reasoned from, the rationale is **checkable against the inputs** — a reviewer can confirm "preferred A over B because A is the primary trial-registry entry and B a secondary news summary" against the actual `source_class` values. The rationale is a **claim to verify, not ground truth** (post-hoc rationalization risk per stream #1) — so it is advisory and the binding gate stays `strict_verify`.\n\nThis is the §-1.1-compliant story: no metadata/count/string-presence signal, every weight traceable to a cited span and an inspectable reason.\n\n### 1.4 Reliability and where it fails (and the guardrail)\n\nStream #1 is blunt about the failure modes, and they bound the design: LLMs "treat all retrieved documents as equally valid" unless explicitly steered and over-rely on high-frequency claims (arXiv 2505.17762); AuthorityBench (2603.25092) shows authority perception is **inconsistent across domains and highly prompt-sensitive**, struggles with false authority, and exhibits position/verbosity/self-preference bias. Therefore:\n\n- **The skill is ADVISORY to ranking/selection, never the faithfulness gate.** It changes *which rows are billed/weighted*, not *whether a sentence verifies its span*. `strict_verify`, 4-role/two-family segregation, and the independent `corpus_approval_gate` tier-distribution backstop all still run sequentially.\n- **Bias mitigations baked into the prompt:** score rationale-first then number; randomize candidate order to blunt position bias; run pointwise (per-source) to avoid listwise permutation instability.\n- **Offline validation before trust:** the chosen judge model+prompt is validated on an **AuthorityBench-style harness** (authority-PERCEPTION only, per the §6a no-overstatement correction — not as a volume-vulnerability benchmark) plus the existing vax adversarial fixture, before it is allowed on the shipping path. Calibration (Brier/ECE) on the emitted weights runs in the existing Phase 11 harness.\n- **Capture resistance:** multi-signal, self-computed, verifier-overridable — no NewsGuard/MBFC/Ad Fontes verdict is hardwired as ground truth (the FTC-capture risk).\n\n### 1.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L1\n\n**HAVE:** deterministic authority signals (`authority_model`, `tier_classifier`), the additive `AuthorityResult`/`SentenceVerification` schema, independence-collapse, `scope_templates` domain detection, the JSON-verdict schema discipline.\n**MISSING:** the generic per-question credibility skill itself, and the second (relevance) axis.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 2a/2b):**\n1. `authority/credibility_skill.py` — the pure 3-step (query→source→two-axis-judgment) prompt module emitting `{reliability, relevance, credibility_weight, rationale, signals_cited}`. Domain is an input *hint*, not a rubric switch. (Replaces the fixed-rubric selector in `credibility_prior.py`.)\n2. Add `relevance_score` (and the rationale fields) to `AuthorityResult` (`source_class.py:75`); emit on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n3. Wire the skill's weight into the L5 weight-mass tally and the L7 disclosure fields — advisory input, not a gate.\n4. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; OFF = the legacy single-scalar path, byte-for-byte.\n\n---\n\n## 2. LAYER 6 (composition + UI): neutral multi-perspective + transparent evidence-weight artifact, replacing the forewarning/fringe framing\n\n### 2.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 7 (L6)** composes contested claims as "consensus at high weight + minority attributed at low weight **with explicit forewarning**, medical-misinfo **low-weight/fringe**, ABSTAIN over fabricated balance." The operator directive is to **replace the forewarning/fringe framing** with a **neutral multi-perspective presentation where competing world-views are shown as legitimate, each carrying its real (possibly asymmetric) evidence weight, and the user judges** — no bias, no stamps, no forewarning prose.\n\n### 2.2 The hard tension, named and resolved (do not skip this)\n\nThere is a real conflict between two binding constraints, and both sides have a lethal failure mode:\n\n- The operator wants **no fringe/forewarning stamp, all sides legitimate, no bias.**\n- The **clinical-safety invariant #5** (binding, preserved) plus Dixon-Clarke 2013 (PubMed 23193194, measured harm: falsely-balanced vaccine reporting *reduced* perceived safety and vaccination intent), Wikipedia FRINGE, and §-1.1 all say **equal treatment of unequal evidence is itself bias** and is "lethal in clinical." I am not authorized to drop invariant #5.\n\n**The reconciliation — handed to us nearly verbatim by all three streams: replace the LABEL with the BASIS.** Wikipedia NPOV's "neutral *editing*, not neutral *content*"; GRADE's "show the basis, let the user infer"; the no-warning rule from stream #2 is satisfiable **if and only if the criteria behind any weight are displayed**. So:\n\n- The **system** stamps nothing — no "fringe," no "debunked," no forewarning sentence. That is the neutrality.\n- The **evidence weight** carries the (often asymmetric) truth, rendered as a visible **per-perspective evidence-basis panel** the user reads and judges. The asymmetry — *14 RCTs + 3 systematic reviews [High]* vs *2 case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]* — becomes **visible in the artifact** instead of **asserted in a stamp**. The user infers; the system does not editorialize.\n- "Legitimate" means **shown with its real (possibly low) weight and one-click drill-down to span + verdict** — NOT "shown as equally supported." A genuine world-view dispute with no empirical consensus (values, forecasting) shows steelmanned sides at honest parity; an empirical dispute with consensus shows the real weight distribution.\n- The medical-misinfo case is handled **without the banned word**: its panel is simply **near-empty of independent high-tier basis**, and where there is no independent consensus the claim **ABSTAINs** ("contested; no independent consensus" — reusing existing refuse/unresolved behavior). This preserves invariant #5's *substance* (not normalized as an equal side) while dropping the stamp.\n\nThis is neutral AND anti-false-balance AND §-1.1-safe simultaneously — the operator's "let the user judge" satisfied by *showing the basis*, not by *equalizing the boxes*.\n\n### 2.3 The exact composition mechanism\n\nPer question, the composition path first **diagnoses the contestation type** (this IS the adaptive guardrail, copied from Anthropic's even-handedness "lack-of-consensus" discriminator + OpenAI's seek-the-truth-together):\n\n- **empirical-with-consensus** (vaccines, climate) → due-weight: each side shown, weight proportional to verified-evidence quality; the asymmetry is the message, carried by the basis panel.\n- **genuine-uncertainty** (open scientific questions) → separate *supported* from *open*; sides at honest parity.\n- **values dispute** (politics, ethics) → **steelman each legitimate view** (Ideological Turing Test, neutral terminology, no verdict); no evidence-weight asymmetry asserted because there is no due weight to defer to.\n- **forecasting** (AI-and-jobs) → scenario spread, projective not factual.\n\nEach perspective is gathered through its **own retrieval lens** (MODS-style per-perspective retrieval + citation, arXiv 2502.00322 — raw LLMs silently drop minority views; per-perspective retrieval prevents the drop), then composed via the **existing 4-role / voter-arbiter agreement** (cross-lineage, open-weight voters — the bridging/balanced-panel analog) — **not a new adjudication path**. Reranking-for-balance (arXiv 2506.15925) selects the most balanced faithful candidate, because direct just-prompt-balanced generation empirically skews. **The weight attached to each perspective is evidence QUALITY × independent-origin count, never document count** — else we rebuild Ground News's source-count bias inside the summarizer.\n\n### 2.4 The friendly UI artifact — design\n\nBuilt on the **components that already exist** (#744 citation chip + hover source card, #745 per-claim verdict chip, #747 Proof Replay split-view, #757 report page, #758 claim-anchored compare view) — not designed from scratch.\n\n**The artifact: a claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** that opens from any contested sentence (reusing the compare-view host), containing per legitimate perspective:\n\n1. **A neutral position header** — "Position held by [who], with this evidence behind it" — no "fringe"/"warning"/"debunked". A steelmanned one-line statement of the view in neutral terms.\n2. **An evidence-basis sub-panel (GRADE-style, the load-bearing element):** the study types / source tiers / independent-origin count behind that perspective, rendered as **frequencies, not a percentage or a lone dot** — "*8 of n: RCTs + systematic reviews [High certainty]*" vs "*2 of n: case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]*". The certainty label is acceptable **only because its downgrade criteria are shown** (study design / consistency / directness / independence). This makes the asymmetry self-evident without a stamp.\n3. **Scite-style polarity badges** (supporting / contrasting / contextualizing) on each cited source — **neutral hue + icon + text label, never color alone** (colorblind safety; red=contrasting reads as red=wrong and biases toward consensus — explicitly avoided).\n4. **Drill-down:** every badge, every basis count, every panel is **one click from the raw `[#ev]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict** — the Scite "self-correcting" property: a mis-classified badge is exposed by the citing sentence sitting right behind it, and a meter without drill-down would violate the §-1.1 metadata-audit ban.\n\n**Panel sizing defeats false balance structurally, not with a caveat:** panels are **sized/annotated by real verified-evidence weight**, not equal-width — because equal real estate reads as equal credibility (the false-balance trap), and a caveat is empirically insufficient (Effectiviology / false-balance literature). The asymmetry is *in the layout and the basis panel*, not asserted in prose.\n\n**Three named anti-patterns explicitly rejected** (all from the streams, all as negative templates):\n- **Ground News source-count / coverage bar** — measures who-is-saying-this (volume), not evidence weight; §-1.1 bans source counts as quality signals. If a composition strip is shown at all, its axis is **evidence tier / source type**, never ideology or headcount, and it is kept visually separate from the weight panel so "many sources" never reads as "true."\n- **Bare confidence/credibility score (% or traffic-light dot)** — the best-documented HARM pattern: overconfident displays (80% shown / 70% accurate) pushed AI-advice adoption 57%→69.6%, 64% of users mis-judged calibration, and in a 2025 breast-cancer CDSS high confidence *reduced* diagnostic accuracy via overreliance. POLARIS's honest replacement is the per-sentence VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict against a real span — never a glanceable dot.\n- **Symmetric 50/50 equal-box presentation** — manufactures false balance; the Dixon-Clarke measured harm.\n\n### 2.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L6\n\n**HAVE:** the disclosure substrate (per-sentence `[#ev]` tokens, `strict_verify` verdict ladder, `SentenceVerification` additive fields), the existing 4-role/voter-arbiter agreement, independence-collapse for origin-count, and the full UI component kit (citation chip, verdict chip, Proof Replay, compare view).\n**MISSING:** the contestation-type classifier, per-perspective retrieval + composition that defeats false balance by weight, and the Perspectives artifact rendering basis panels.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 7):**\n1. A **contestation-type classifier** (empirical-consensus / genuine-uncertainty / values / forecasting) priming the composition rule — replaces the fixed forewarning+fringe rule.\n2. **Per-perspective retrieval + balance-rerank composition** over the post-collapse origins, weighting each perspective by verified-evidence quality × independent-origin count (not count).\n3. A **steelman + GRADE-style evidence-basis renderer** feeding a new **claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** in the compare-view host: neutral headers, frequency-framed basis sub-panels, Scite-style neutral polarity badges, full span-verdict drill-down, weight-proportional sizing.\n4. Validated with a **paired-prompt neutrality eval** (Anthropic's open-sourced political-neutrality harness, adapted so it does NOT reward equal treatment of unequal evidence) + the vax adversarial fixture (asserts the asymmetry renders, no stamp, no 50/50).\n5. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; ABSTAIN reuses existing refuse/unresolved behavior; forewarning prose removed, replaced by the basis panel.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Consolidated "build" summary\n\n| Layer | Replaces (2026-06-07 plan) | POLARIS builds | Binding gate untouched |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **L1 scoring** | Fixed 3-rubric domain selector (Phase 2a/2b) | `credibility_skill.py` — generic 3-step SIFT/lateral-reading skill, reliability×relevance, rationale bound to `AuthorityResult`/`[#ev]`; domain as hint not switch; `relevance_score` axis | `strict_verify` binding; skill advisory; corpus_approval backstop |\n| **L6 composition+UI** | Forewarning + fringe framing (Phase 7) | Contestation-type classifier + per-perspective retrieval/balance-rerank weighted by quality×origin-count + steelman/GRADE-basis "Perspectives" panel (replace label with basis) | 4-role/voter-arbiter reused (no parallel path); ABSTAIN reuses refuse/unresolved; invariant #5 substance preserved |\n\nHonest residual: L1's skill is prompt-sensitive and must clear offline AuthorityBench-style + calibration validation before it is trusted on the shipping path; L6's steelman is the hardest element to audit mechanically (no test certifies an argument is the strongest version vs a disguised strawman — the paired-prompt eval is the best available check, itself a judgment). Both remain advisory; `strict_verify` carries faithfulness.\n\n---\n\n## 4. .\docs\agent_architecture.md:3:> **ARCHITECTURE DNA (operator-locked 2026-06-13, CLAUDE.md §-1.3):** The pipeline is **WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, not FILTER-AND-CAP.** (1) **Weight** every relevant source by credibility — don't filter it out (social media stays at low weight; the tier classifier IS the weighting system). (2) **Consolidate** same-claim sources into a basket and keep them ALL as multi-citation corroboration. (3) Decide **faithfulness against the whole basket**, never one span (single-source verification is a blind spot). The faithfulness engine (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; everything else is a weight or a consolidation. **Banned:** hardcoded caps/targets/thinners to force a breadth number. **Surgical, not rewrite.** See CLAUDE.md §-1.3, `docs/polaris_pipeline_canonical.md`, `docs/pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md`, I-arch-001 (#1245).

.\docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:28:**The single most expensive belief POLARIS is currently holding that this research overturns:** the operator-locked DeepSeek V4 Pro generator is the WRONG generator for the hallucination-sensitivity dimension. Vectara HHEM-2.3 evaluator (May 2026 leaderboard) ranks V4 Pro at 8.6%, V3.2-Exp at 5.3%, Qwen3-8B at 4.8%, Antgroup Finix S1 32B at 1.8%. All four lower-hallucination options are open-weight non-US. V4 Pro is also notorious for 94% non-abstention on AA-Omniscience (it answers when it doesn't know). The 44.4% refusal rate POLARIS is seeing is V4 Pro's low-abstention pathology being correctly caught by POLARIS's validator. This is a generator-choice problem, not a validator problem. **Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 + `feedback_route_policy_questions_to_codex.md` I am surfacing this for Codex review, not silently switching.** Operator's "frontier-only, top-notch only" directive was a general capability directive; the dimension-specific data argues for an additional consideration. Most defensible path: keep V4 Pro as primary reasoning generator AND add Qwen3-8B as a parallel "low-hallucination prose finalizer" model in the same ensemble used by CHECK, exploiting the family diversity that POLARIS's two-family invariant (§9.1) already requires.
.\docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:38:2. **Abstention discipline (refusal templates when no atom can be cited).** MedHallu (Feb 2025) [^medhallu] empirically demonstrated that **"adding an 'abstention / not sure' option enhances precision by up to 15% for larger models; GPT-4o achieves 79.5% F1 with this feature."** POLARIS's 44.4% refusal rate is the system correctly exercising the abstention discipline; the academic literature treats this as a feature, not a bug. The Carney pitch should frame refusal rate as a safety credibility lever, not apologise for it.

.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:155:**Perplexity (Sonar models + Deep Research).** Real-time web grounding, cites 50 sources per Deep Research report, achieves **93.9% on SimpleQA, 21.1% on Humanity's Last Exam** [^perplexity_2026]. **Critically: Perplexity does NOT independently fact-check the sources it cites — it relies entirely on source credibility, propagating bias from top search results into output** [^perplexity_2026]. Citation hallucination rate per the Columbia Journalism Review audit: 37% (lowest among major search platforms — but still >1 in 3 citations potentially fabricated or misdirected) [^perplexity_2026]. **This is the key competitive insight: Perplexity is fast and source-rich, but its faithfulness layer is weaker than POLARIS's atom-grounded design when POLARIS is working correctly.**
.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:395:[^perplexity_2026]: Suprmind, [Perplexity AI 2026: Models, Features, Pricing, and Citation Accuracy](https://suprmind.ai/hub/perplexity/). 93.9% SimpleQA. .\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:2:### State of the art on source diversity, per-source credibility weighting, weighted synthesis, contested-topic handling, echo-chamber detection, and per-claim disclosure — with the POLARIS lead opportunity
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:8:There is no deployed system today that does credibility-weighted, both-sides deep research well. The frontier products (OpenAI/Gemini/Perplexity Deep Research) are agentic and broad but treat credibility as prose narration or a clickable link list — never a disclosed weight. The scientific-evidence tools (Elicit, Consensus, scite, Semantic Scholar, Exa, STORM) mostly manufacture "credibility" by **restricting the corpus to peer-reviewed papers** — which is precisely the journal-only filter POLARIS is abandoning — and the ones that touch the open web (Exa, STORM) handle credibility badly. The formal evidence-synthesis frameworks (GRADE, GRADE-CERQual, Cochrane/Campbell, EFSA/OHAT weight-of-evidence, the Maryland SMS) get the *methodology* right — weight, never count — but assume trained human reviewers and curated studies, not an automated pipeline ingesting the whole web. And the credibility-aware RAG literature (RA-RAG, CAG, RAGRank) has working weighted-aggregation algorithms but keeps the weights internal and, critically, mostly **assumes source independence** — the one thing the contested hard case breaks.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:12:1. **None of the three frontier DR products publishes a numeric per-source credibility weight, a per-claim evidence-strength score, or any echo-chamber / source-independence detection.** OpenAI narrates credibility in prose; Gemini and Perplexity give you a link list. That absence is not a gap to paper over — it is the differentiation space for POLARIS's per-sentence, span-bound provenance.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:18:4. **The "credibility" of the evidence tools is mostly corpus restriction.** Their per-claim signal *mechanics* are transferable (scite's support/contrast classification, Elicit's span-linked extraction, GRADE's certainty label); their corpus-restriction *strategy* is exactly what POLARIS rejects and must not inherit.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:22:The through-line: **quality is WEIGHT, not COUNT, and the weight must (a) actually drive synthesis, (b) discount non-independent corroboration, and (c) be disclosed per claim.** No deployed system does all three. POLARIS's existing per-sentence `[#ev:id:start-end]` provenance + `strict_verify` is the right substrate; the missing layers are a disclosed credibility prior, an independence/echo-chamber collapse, and a per-claim weight-and-disclose UX.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:30:Each issue below covers the seven POLARIS dimensions: (a) source diversity vs filtering, (b) per-source credibility weighting + signals, (c) composition/synthesis weighted by credibility, (d) contested-topic both-sides handling (the vax case), (e) volume-vs-weight + source-independence/echo-chamber detection, (f) per-claim credibility disclosure. For each, what the frontier DOES, the SOTA best practice, and the OUTCOME (how well, known failures).
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:36:**(a) Source diversity vs filtering.** All three are breadth-first open-web agents with no categorical source-type filter by default. OpenAI uniquely adds an *opt-in* trusted-sites allowlist plus authenticated/paid connectors (FactSet, PitchBook, Drive/SharePoint) as of Feb 2026. Gemini defaults to Google Search + optional Workspace content and markets reach to "academic journals, government databases, reputable news" — but "reputable" is marketing framing, not a documented filter. Perplexity is open-web agentic RAG. **Diversity is high; filtering is essentially absent or user-manual.** This validates POLARIS's all-source-type direction — but none of them adds a credibility layer on top of the breadth.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:38:**(b) Per-source credibility weighting.** *No published numeric weight in any of the three.* OpenAI's marketing claim that the agent "actively evaluates the credibility of sources" and "explains why each source was chosen" resolves on inspection to **prose narration in the reasoning trace** — not a score, not a signal set, not an auditable weight. The only real OpenAI lever is the manual allowlist. Gemini's own docs are silent on credibility weighting (confirmed by fetch); the Sources panel is a manual visual scan. Perplexity markets "credibility as the primary filter" but every concrete description (five-stage gauntlet, L1–L3 rerankers) is **third-party SEO/GEO blog reverse-engineering, not vendor documentation** — and Perplexity's official Deep Research blog was 403 during research, so its internals could not be primary-verified. **SOTA best practice here is, frankly, the absence — there is no frontier best practice to copy.**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:40:**(c) Composition weighted by credibility.** Not documented by any of the three. Inferred behavior from ReportBench: OpenAI DR does heavy citation *post-processing* — 88.2 vs 16.16 cited statements per query vs the bare o3 model, and the highest citation precision (~0.385 vs Gemini ~0.145). So composition optimizes for **citation density and coverage, not credibility-weighted synthesis.** Gemini "generates excessive citations without proportional improvement in coverage" — volume-heavy, not weight-disciplined.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:42:**(d) Contested-topic / the vax case.** **All three FALSE-CONVERGE to one-sidedness, not false-balance.** DeepTRACE one-sidedness on debate queries: GPT-5(DR) 54.67% (best/lowest, yet still a majority), Perplexity(DR) 63.1%, Gemini(DR) 80.1% (second-worst). None has a documented weight-and-disclose policy for the vaccine-vs-antivaccine more-sources-but-lower-credibility case. Behavior is emergent from model training, not a transparent both-sides policy. **The failure mode is silent side-picking + under-support, which is arguably more dangerous than naive 50/50 because it is invisible.**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:46:**(f) Per-claim credibility disclosure.** All three provide inline per-claim citations (click-through to source). **None shows per-claim evidence strength or source-quality score, and none shows whether the cited span actually supports the claim** — verification is left to the reader. Perplexity's numbered chips are the signature UX yet are "largely decorative given the 97.5% unsupported-statement rate."
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:55:**Relevance to POLARIS:** OpenAI DR is the closest competitor on citation hygiene and the system to beat on faithfulness. Its "explains why each source was chosen" is prose, not an auditable weight — exactly what POLARIS's disclosed credibility prior + span-bound provenance token out-rigors. The 21% unsupported-citation rate and the 79%→17% degradation curve are the concrete failure modes `strict_verify` (numeric match + content-word span overlap) is built to catch. **Perplexity is the strongest single argument FOR the POLARIS thesis:** most citation-visible product, grounds almost nothing — proof that visible citations without span verification are theater.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:63:- **SPLIT mechanics from corpus strategy.** With one exception (Exa), every tool here manufactures credibility by *restricting the corpus* to academic/peer-reviewed papers (OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, ~125–280M scholarly works). That corpus restriction IS the journal-only filter POLARIS is abandoning. Adopt the **claim-support mechanics**; reject the **corpus-restriction strategy**.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:67:**(a) Source diversity vs filtering.** scite (~280M scholarly works, ~1.4B citation statements), Consensus (OpenAlex + Semantic Scholar ~150–200M), Elicit (~125M+), Semantic Scholar (~200M papers / 2.4B citation links) are all corpus-restricted to academia. **Exa is THE exception** — a general neural web-search engine over tens of billions of pages, explicitly not SEO-influenced (paid per query), the closest model to POLARIS's all-source ingestion. STORM is open-web but *outsources* credibility to an upstream search engine (You.com/Bing "trusted sources") and never evaluates trust itself.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:69:**(b) Per-source credibility weighting + signals — what's transferable:**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:70:- **scite (HIGH transferable):** Does NOT assign a source credibility weight; its signal is orthogonal and more interesting — per-citation-STATEMENT intent classification (supporting / contrasting-disputing / mentioning) via a SciBERT classifier with a confidence %, plus retraction/editorial-notice flags. "Credibility" here = *downstream reception* (how later papers treated the claim), span-anchored and disclosed.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:74:- **Exa (LOW as credibility model):** Internal LLM-grader "result_quality = authority/accuracy/trustworthiness," used to tune ranking but **never surfaced to the user.**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:76:**(c) Composition weighted by credibility.** Mostly vote-counting. Consensus classifies the top 5–20 results into Yes/No/Possibly/Mixed and visualizes the distribution — quality tags shown alongside but **do not re-weight the tally.** scite aggregates N-supporting vs N-contrasting counts. Elicit composes a span-hyperlinked matrix (no auto prominence-by-credibility). STORM synthesizes by *perspective coverage*, not credibility weight — the most synthesis-heavy tool and the one whose credibility handling is weakest.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:88:- **STORM:** Vendor Co-STORM "~99% factual" vs peer-reviewed STORM **~85% citation precision/recall** — a marketing-vs-eval gap that is itself a credibility signal. Named open problems: source-bias transfer, over-speculation, non-neutral tone.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:95:### ISSUE 3 — Source-credibility scoring orgs + credibility-aware RAG + independence detection (NewsGuard, MBFC, Ad Fontes, RA-RAG, CAG, RAGRank, content-sharing networks, Community Notes, weight-of-evidence/MEDRS)
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:101:**(b) Per-source credibility weighting + signals:**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:105:- **RA-RAG (the weighted-aggregation blueprint):** Estimates per-source reliability **without labels** via Dawid-Skene/EM cross-source agreement, then **Weighted Majority Voting** — the answer with the most reliability-weight wins, so a few high-credibility sources override a larger low-credibility bloc. Estimated reliabilities correlated with ground truth at PCC 0.991 / SRCC 0.992.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:106:- **CAG ("Not All Contexts Are Equal"):** Attaches credibility tags (high/med/low) to each context and **fine-tunes the synthesizer to weight by them** — because off-the-shelf LLMs show "limited sensitivity to credibility" when merely prompted. Survives ~80% noise (~91.7% accuracy vs baselines ~78%).
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:109:**(c) Composition weighted by credibility.** RA-RAG's Weighted Majority Voting and CAG's credibility-deference are the two cleanest operationalizations of "credibility governs which claim wins a conflict." RAGRank filters low-trust sources pre-retrieval. The rating orgs are external priors consumed downstream.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:122:**(f) Per-claim disclosure.** The entire rating-org industry is **source-level, not per-claim** — NewsGuard says so explicitly ("rates entire websites, not individual articles or claims"). RA-RAG and CAG keep weights internal (CAG's annotations stay in the *input*; the answer never surfaces which sources it prioritized). Community Notes is the closest to claim-level (per-post note + sources) but discloses a status, not a graded weight. **Per-claim credibility disclosure is essentially unsolved across this entire issue — confirming it is POLARIS's whitespace.**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:126:- **AuthorityBench (arXiv 2603.25092):** Independently shows current LLMs suffer **"volume vulnerability"** (adopt consistent-but-false claims from many low-credibility sources over few high-credibility) and **"credibility ignorance"** (treat all sources as equal) — a measured, labeled benchmark of exactly the failure POLARIS must beat.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:140:**(b) Per-source credibility weighting + signals:**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:144:- **Maryland SMS:** 5-point design-validity scale (1=correlation … 5=RCT), quasi-experiments (diff-in-diff, RD, IV) in the strong middle. Per the economics "credibility revolution" (Card/Angrist/Imbens 2021 Nobel), **a strong natural experiment can be near-top evidence — RCTs are NOT automatically the apex outside clinical efficacy.**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:146:**(c) Composition weighted by credibility — concrete, auditable primitives:**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:159:**Relevance to POLARIS:** The single most important design claim in the entire report comes from here: **there is no universal evidence hierarchy.** The same meta-framework (confidence via explicit domains) must use different hierarchies and different *primary credibility axes* per domain — clinical weights RCTs + peer-review; **economics/policy weights design-validity over venue** (a Nobel-validated shift); qualitative weights coherence/adequacy. **POLARIS's current journal-allowlist `tier_classifier` would mis-rank a strong NBER working-paper natural experiment (routed to policy/think-tank T4) below a weak peer-reviewed cross-sectional study.** A global journal-only filter is methodologically wrong outside clinical efficacy questions. Adopt: separate SOURCE-credibility prior from EVIDENCE-certainty posterior; reliability×relevance two-axis weight; "lowest-among-critical" + inverse-variance as auditable non-averaging primitives; domain-conditional rubrics driven off the existing `scope_templates`; grey literature as first-class weighted by design.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:171:**(c) Composition.** Per-statement support checking, ablation, Citation-F1, scite support/contrast/mention, Consensus proportion meter — all *measurement* of support, none *weighting-by-credibility.*
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:192:**The ideal outcome.** For any research question, in any domain, ingesting all source types: (1) retrieve broadly without a venue filter; (2) assign each source a *decomposed, disclosed* credibility prior on the correct domain-conditional axis (design-validity for econ/policy, peer-review+RoB for clinical, coherence/adequacy for qualitative); (3) **collapse non-independent sources to independent origins** so 50 sites copying one press release count as ~1; (4) aggregate by reliability-WEIGHT not count, using auditable non-averaging primitives; (5) on contested topics, present the consensus side at its high weight AND the minority side attributed at its (low) weight with an explicit forewarning that the minority has more-but-weaker sources — never censor, never false-balance; (6) attach to every sentence a span-bound provenance token, a certainty label, a credibility weight, and an independent-origin count — and let the user replay the proof.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:196:- **Disclosed per-claim credibility weight: unsolved everywhere.** Frontier products narrate or list; rating orgs are source-level only; RA-RAG and CAG keep weights internal. This is open whitespace.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:198:- **The contested low-credibility-majority (vax) hard case: unsolved.** Academic tools avoid it by corpus restriction; open-web tools (Exa, STORM) handle it badly (echo-amplification or false balance); frontier products silently pick one side and under-support it. The *normative* answer (weight-of-evidence + forewarning + bridging) is known and even empirically tested, but **no automated system implements weight-and-disclose-with-forewarning.**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:205:**Where POLARIS could LEAD, given per-claim provenance.** POLARIS already has the hardest substrate — per-sentence `[#ev:id:start-end]` tokens + `strict_verify` (numeric match + ≥2 content-word span overlap) + zero-verified-abort. That is the verify-against-span layer the entire faithfulness literature says is missing and the layer a citation chip cannot provide. The lead is to extend that substrate from *provenance disclosure* to **provenance + credibility-weight + independent-origin-count + certainty disclosure per claim** — the union that no competitor offers — and to make the credibility weight (a) domain-conditional, (b) independence-aware, and (c) actually drive composition (not sit beside it as a decoration like Consensus's chips).
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:213:**Layer 0 — Retrieve broadly, validated by Exa.** Keep all-source-type semantic retrieval (Exa proves a non-journal-filtered retriever is viable). Treat retrieval as ingest; add the credibility/independence/disclosure layers Exa stops short of.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:215:**Layer 1 — Domain-conditional, two-axis, decomposed credibility prior (replace the single-tier conflation).**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:216:- Detect the question's domain off the existing `scope_templates`, then select the *primary credibility axis*: clinical → RCT-apex + peer-review + RoB proxy; **economics/policy → design-validity (SMS-style), NOT venue** (a strong NBER natural experiment outranks a weak peer-reviewed cross-section); qualitative → CERQual coherence/adequacy/relevance.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:228:- For discrete factual claims: RA-RAG-style **Weighted Majority Voting over the post-collapse independent origins** — a few high-credibility sources override a larger low-credibility bloc.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:237:- Carry scite's **retraction/editorial-notice flag as a hard credibility penalty**, and **favor RECALL on the refutes/contrasting class** (over-detect potential contradiction, fail loud / route to human — a missed refutation is the lethal clinical error; this is the *opposite* of scite's precision-favoring tune).
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:240:- Extend the per-sentence `[#ev:id:start-end]` token so each verified sentence carries **{span-verdict, credibility weight, independent-origin count "N sources → M origins", certainty label}** — the union of Elicit's traceability + scite's intent signal + GRADE's certainty + a weight none of them show.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:245:- Benchmark against **AuthorityBench** (volume-vulnerability + credibility-ignorance) and RA-RAG's Adversary-Hammer prior — labeled tests where naive volume-based synthesis flips to the false majority — to *prove* weight beats count on the vax-shaped case.
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:280:**Credibility scoring + credibility-aware RAG + independence detection:**
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:303:- Economics credibility revolution — https://www.nber.org/papers/w15794 ; https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4567672 ; https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6680130/
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:323:- CAG is EMNLP 2024 (not 2025); its "credibility" labels are largely relevance/timeliness/noise, not source authority; 0.917 is exact-match on EvolvingTempQA at noise 0.8. (arxiv 2404.06809)
.\docs\frontier_credibility_intelligence_2026_06_07.md:330:- Competitor mapping (add): Claude Research + Claude web search + Anthropic Citations API; Microsoft 365 Copilot Researcher/Analyst (wraps OpenAI deep research); Grok DeepSearch/DeeperSearch. .\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:9:3. A **WEIGHTING system** → score each kept source by credibility (peer-reviewed? high-reputation journal? gov? institute? working paper? news? social media?). **WEIGHT, do NOT filter-out.** Social media STAYS (low weight; sometimes it reports a real journal).
.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:21:**The pipeline's only claim-consolidation mechanism _drops_ the corroborating sources and keeps a single representative row plus an integer count, while credibility weight is used only to rank-then-drop and to write one corpus-level advisory mean — so there is no per-claim "supported by X, Y, Z, each with weight W" surface anywhere in the running pipeline. Every drop/cap/thin/target knob below is a symptom of that missing surface: each one forces a breadth NUMBER instead of weighting + consolidating + presenting all relevant sources.**
.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:43:  ── weighted-corpus gate (I-cred-006b #1170, ON): writes ONE corpus-level weighted_credibility_mean,
.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:44:     ADVISORY, to corpus_credibility_disclosure.json — NOT propagated per-citation
.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:62:**Divergence A — weight is a rank-then-drop key, never a per-claim surface.** The deterministic `authority_score` (from OpenAlex enrich) is used ONLY as (a) a RANK key — `_relevance_floor_selection` sorts by `-(relevance × authority_score)` (`evidence_selector.py:1624-1630`); `finding_dedup` ranks representatives by `authority_score` (`finding_dedup.py:156-162`) — and (b) a single corpus-level ADVISORY mean (`weighted_corpus_gate.py`, written to `corpus_credibility_disclosure.json`). The architecture wants each CLAIM presented as "supported by X, Y, Z each with weight W." The running pipeline instead uses weight to rank, then **drops** everything below the lexical relevance floor, and writes one corpus-wide number. **There is no per-claim multi-source weighted attribution surfaced to the user.** This is the spine divergence.
.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:66:**Divergence C — the live drop is the relevance floor, not the consolidation.** `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR=0.30` (ON in the Gate-B slate) hard-drops every row below 0.30 lexical relevance. The in-file forensic comment (`evidence_selector.py:1316`) records it cut **236 of 589 rows (589 → 353)** on a live run, and a parallel drb_76 trace shows **597 → 53**. A clean median relevance of ~0.50 means a 0.30 floor still kills the on-topic tail. Lexical/embedding relevance is exactly what should DOWN-WEIGHT a source, not delete it. This is the headline live damage on drb_72.
.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:77:- **→ WEIGHT** — a hard drop that should become a credibility weight in the per-claim presentation (the source STAYS, ranked lower).

.\docs\pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md:225:**The faithfulness engine is the ONLY hard gate that stays.** strict_verify, NLI entailment, the 4-role D8 audit, and the `[#ev:id:start-end]` provenance binding are untouched by every issue in §5. Every weighting/consolidation change can only (a) keep a source that the old floor/cap dropped, (b) change its credibility WEIGHT, or (c) change how it is DISPLAYED per claim. None of these is an input to the span-grounding verdict. A claim still cannot appear unless it is span-grounded; the migration only ensures that ALL the sources which support a span-grounded claim are surfaced to the user with their weights, instead of being dropped to hit a breadth number. Weight and consolidation move a source's VISIBILITY and RANK; only faithfulness moves its ADMISSIBILITY.

.\docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:125:`confidence_bucket` rule (clinical-safety invariant): **a non-VERIFIED claim can NEVER render `high`.** `high` requires `verdict==VERIFIED AND credibility>=high AND origins>=2`. Unknown credibility → `low` (never inflates). Zero resolvable cited evidence → `no-source-found`. The bucket is a **pure deterministic function** of the fields above — no LLM call, no spend.

.\docs\permanent_fix_migration_blueprint.md:349:3. **Credibility-as-weight + `independent_origin_count` — can echoing low-credibility sources fake "moderate (3 sources)"?** RA-RAG downweights but RETAINS low-reliability sources. .\docs\polaris_fundamental_rearchitecture_plan.md:11:POLARIS becomes a domain-general, globally-sourced, verifiable deep-research engine that produces top-notch reports for an expert in **any field, any region** — physics, law, agriculture, African energy policy, Japanese pharma regulation — because the only domain signal in the system is **the question itself**, never a curated host list, per-slug answer key, or `if domain ==` branch. Source reach and credibility come from field-agnostic *computed signals* (citation-graph authority, structural primary-vs-secondary class, issuer self-description, independent corroboration), not enumeration. The faithfulness wedge that the frontier lacks — #13 citation faithfulness, #14 numeric-verbatim grounding, #17 fail-closed refusal, #19 signed audit/replay, #23 open-weight sovereignty — is preserved *unchanged in guarantee* through every redesign and is *extended* to cover computed trade-off numbers, which is strictly stronger than ChatGPT/Gemini DR.

.\docs\process\polaris_master_blueprint_2026_05_28.md:51:What does a correct dry run produce for review? It produces one review dossier per question, in two synchronized forms so both auditors can work line by line: a machine-readable trace with one line per claim, and the same rows rendered as readable markdown. Each claim row carries the Generator sentence, its provenance tag, the Mirror's two passes, the Sentinel score and what its polarity means, the deterministic Python result, the Judge's five-way verdict and its reasoning trace, the release action and whether release was allowed, the memory decision and the reason, and one more field that matters for honesty: whether each fixture was a recorded real response or hand-built from the model's contract. Alongside that go the ordered log lines and the two audit ledgers, one from Claude and one from Codex, written into the existing ledger artifact, not a parallel one. Because the models are mocked and free, the dossier can include things too expensive to run live, like killing the run at every single checkpoint and like adversarial memory fixtures that try to poison the graph. That is why Gate A is genuinely strong, not a consolation prize.

.\docs\telus\telus_pitch_draft.md:324:> `provenance_generator.py:2670` · shipped + unit-tested · parallel path flag-gated (`PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY`, resolver `:2573`; slate sets 20)
.\docs\telus\telus_pitch_draft.md:326:`strict_verify` splits the draft, canonicalizes malformed `[ev:...]` tokens to `[#ev:...]` so a citation is never *silently* lost (`:2736`, I-pipe-015), excludes content-empty fragments and web-crawl boilerplate from BOTH numerator and denominator (so a 404 "Page not found" stub can't self-entail and inflate the verified ratio — `:2744-2756`, BUG-19), and runs `verify_sentence_provenance` per surviving sentence either serially or across a bounded `ThreadPoolExecutor` capped at `PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY` workers. The parallel path is `:2777-2792`: it captures the parent context once (`import contextvars as _ctxvars; _parent_ctx = _ctxvars.copy_context()`, `:2790-2792`) and runs each task in a copy so per-run judge telemetry, ambient role, and provider pin survive the worker thread — and a worker exception **propagates** (fail-loud), never swallowed. Kept/dropped reassembly is index-aligned, so the parallel result is byte-identical to serial. Output: `StrictVerificationReport(total_in, total_kept, total_dropped, ...)`.
.\docs\telus\telus_pitch_draft.md:364:- **Legacy (flag OFF, `{0,false,no,off}`):** the error re-raises (`:443-444`), which in the parallel seam path reaches `sweep_integration`'s `future.result()`, tears the **whole** seam down, and hardcodes `coverage_fraction = 0.0` — discarding the successfully-adjudicated claims and (legacy) holding the entire report. A dropped future must never silently reduce the claim count.

.\docs\telus\telus_edge_survival_verdict.md:12:2. **Signed, replayable per-claim provenance bundle for long-form TEXT** — defensibility **~2/5**. .\web\public\canonical_bundles\v1_canonical_success\sources\ev_015.txt:1:Treatment with tirzepatide led to significant weight loss in adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities (excluding type 2 diabetes), according to results from the phase 3 SURMOUNT-3 and SURMOUNT-4 studies. Tirzepatide is an investigational once-weekly, dual glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist that integrates the actions of both incretins into a single molecule. The multicenter, randomized, double-blind, parallel, placebo-controlled SURMOUNT-3 trial (ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: [NCT04657016](https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04657016?term=NCT04657016&rank=1)) evaluated the efficacy and safety of tirzepatide for 72 weeks after a 12-week intensive lifestyle intervention lead-in period in adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities, excluding type 2 diabetes. At the end of the 12-week period, study participants (N=579) achieved 6.9% mean weight loss and were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive tirzepatide or placebo subcutaneously once weekly. The efficacy estimand (efficacy prior to discontinuation of study drug) results demonstrated that treatment with tirzepatide was associated with an additional 21.1% reduction in body weight over 72 weeks vs a 3.3% weight regain for the placebo group. Over the 72 weeks, 94.4% of patients in the tirzepatide arm achieved at least a 5% body weight reduction from randomization vs 10.7% of those receiving placebo. The total mean body weight re
.\web\public\canonical_bundles\v1_canonical_success\sources\ev_015.txt:5:ide for 72 weeks after a 12-week intensive lifestyle intervention lead-in period in adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities, excluding type 2 diabetes. At the end of the 12-week period, study participants (N=579) achieved 6.9% mean weight loss and were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive tirzepatide or placebo subcutaneously once weekly. The efficacy estimand (efficacy prior to discontinuation of study drug) results demonstrated that treatment with tirzepatide was associated with an additional 21.1% reduction in body weight over 72 weeks vs a 3.3% weight regain for the placebo group. Over the 72 weeks, 94.4% of patients in the tirzepatide arm achieved at least a 5% body weight reduction from randomization vs 10.7% of those receiving placebo. The total mean body weight reduction for patients treated with tirzepatide from study entry after 12 weeks of lifestyle intervention to 72 weeks of treatment was 26.6%. The treatment-regimen estimand (estimated average treatment effect regardless of treatment discontinuation) results demonstrated that tirzepatide was associated with an additional 18.4% reduction in body weight over 72 weeks vs a 2.5% regain with placebo. Moreover, 87.5% of patients in the tirzepatide arm achieved at least a 5% body weight reduction from randomization vs 16.5% of those in the placebo arm. The total mean body weight reduction for patients treated with tirzepatide from study entry after 12 weeks of lifestyle intervention to 72 weeks of treatment was 24.3%. The multicenter, randomized, double-blind, parallel, placebo-controlled SURMOUNT-4 trial (ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: [NCT04660643](https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04660643?term=NCT04660643&rank=1)) evaluated the efficacy and safety of tirz
.\docs\telus\polaris_telus_pitch_main_narrative.md:66:**3. We weigh and combine breadth instead of capping it.** Instead of silently dropping sources to hit a number, POLARIS keeps them, weighs them by credibility, and groups the ones that agree. Agreement becomes corroboration. This is the roadmap piece — partly built, not yet proven end-to-end. We say so plainly.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:35:> **The one hard gate is the faithfulness engine** — `strict_verify` (per-sentence provenance), the NLI entailment judge, the four-role D8 adjudication, and the offline-re-verifiable signed bundle. Upstream of it, the architecture **weights and consolidates** rather than dropping: the credibility/relevance paths carry every fetched source forward to composition by weight, not by a rank-then-drop filter (forced on the run path by the launcher, §6). Retrieval applies a fetch cap on how many candidates are *fetched* — 40 by default, 740 on the run path — so the shape is broad fan-out, focused read; that cap is stated plainly in §6 and §9.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:114:> `provenance_generator.py:2670` · runs today, unit-tested · parallel path flag-gated (`PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY`, resolver `:2573`; the launcher does NOT force this on — default is serial, resolver returns 1 on unset env (`:2566`); 20 only if the VM slate is sourced) · **the live demo certifies it end-to-end (§0)**
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:116:`strict_verify` splits the draft, canonicalizes malformed `[ev:...]` tokens to `[#ev:...]` so a citation is never *silently* lost (`:2736`, I-pipe-015), excludes content-empty fragments and web-crawl boilerplate from BOTH numerator and denominator (so a 404 "Page not found" stub can't self-entail and inflate the verified ratio — `:2744-2756`, BUG-19), and runs `verify_sentence_provenance` per surviving sentence either serially or across a bounded `ThreadPoolExecutor` capped at `PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY` workers. The parallel path is `:2777-2792`: it captures the parent context once (`import contextvars as _ctxvars; _parent_ctx = _ctxvars.copy_context()`, `:2790-2792`) and runs each task in a copy so per-run judge telemetry, ambient role, and provider pin survive the worker thread — and a worker exception **propagates** (fail-loud), never swallowed. Kept/dropped reassembly is index-aligned, so the parallel result is byte-identical to serial **in the no-fault path only**. The parallel-fault race must be disclosed: at this pin the parallel path shares one module-level httpx client (`entailment_judge.py:586-590`) and a worker's force-close (`:129`) can corrupt sibling POSTs — the "Q78 [X509] PEM lib" race fixed post-pin in `f56ba76b` (`threading.local` per-worker clients, present at HEAD `:348`). So "byte-identical" holds only when no worker faults; under a parallel fault at the pin the shared client can be torn out from under siblings. The demo runs **serial** verify (or the #1264-fixed commit), so the race is not reachable. Output: `StrictVerificationReport(total_in, total_kept, total_dropped, ...)`.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:157:- **Legacy (flag OFF, `{0,false,no,off}`):** the error re-raises (`:443-444`), which in the parallel seam path reaches `sweep_integration`'s `future.result()`, tears the **whole** seam down, and hardcodes `coverage_fraction = 0.0` — discarding the ~177 successfully-adjudicated claims and (legacy) holding the entire report. That pre-fix tear-down was the literal cause of the old Q90 emptiness.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:161:The seam itself adds no paid LLM call beyond the role calls; per-claim compute is parallelized with a bounded pool that reduces in **original claim order** and propagates any worker exception fail-closed (`sweep_integration.py:294-360`, `:600`). A dropped future must never silently reduce the claim count.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:207:This is the section an architecture-DNA-aware reviewer will probe hardest, and the honest answer is nuanced. The operator-locked DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3) is **WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, not FILTER-AND-CAP**: every source flows to composition carrying a credibility weight; same-claim sources consolidate into a basket; faithfulness is judged against the whole basket. That is the target. Here is exactly where the live code stands against it.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:219:- `credibility_pass.py` + `credibility_judge_caller.py` (the credibility pass / judge),
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:220:- `synthesis/credibility_pass.py` and `nodes/weighted_corpus_gate.py` (the latter explicitly *separate* from this machinery).
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:305:- **The default relevance scorer is lexical token-overlap.** `PG_RELEVANCE_SCORER` defaults to `lexical` (`evidence_selector.py:599`); the `semantic_v2` embedding scorer is force-ON **only** on the `run_gate_b.py` path (`:625`). On any non-launcher path the relevance gate is keyword-overlap — junk-permissive (a keyword-stuffed content farm scores high) and synonym-hostile (drb_76 shed 74 on-topic Tier-1 papers, `:493-503`). strict_verify enforces span-bound evidence checks but does not prove source credibility; a single lexically-relevant page with a real matching substring can ship as a grounded finding today.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:384:This is the cross-cutting question and we answer it once, consistently. At the pin: the entailment-judge clients share one module-level httpx client (`entailment_judge.py:586-590`, `self._client`), so if parallel verify were on and a worker's total-deadline fired, `client.close()` (`:129`) would corrupt sibling POSTs — the "Q78 [X509] PEM lib" race fixed post-pin in `f56ba76b`. At HEAD that race is fixed (`threading.local()` per-worker clients, `entailment_judge.py:348`) **but** the production lock at HEAD carries the GLM-5.2 experiment arm (`generator: z-ai/glm-5.2`, `mirror: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`, commit `f877a815`), which makes `verify_lock_against_code` raise `LockMismatch` and is explicitly marked do-not-merge-to-production.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:386:So neither raw tree is the demo target. **The demo target is a clean, operator-signed commit that combines the #1264 client-isolation fix (so parallel verify is race-free) with the experiment arm reverted (so the lock matches the deepseek/glm/minimax/qwen slate and the drift gate is green); the handover preflight names that exact commit before the run is presented as proof.** Two belt-and-suspenders facts make this safe regardless: (1) `PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY` is **not** in `run_gate_b.py`'s forced flags, so on the launcher path verification is **serial** unless the gitignored slate is sourced — the race is not even reachable on the default launcher path; and (2) the demo runs serial verify, which sidesteps the shared-client race entirely. This is a pre-demo operator action item: pick the clean commit, revert the arm, confirm serial verify. §9 carries the HEAD≠pin / lock-drift caveat so a reviewer at the branch tip is not surprised.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:416:The precise scope: **strict_verify enforces span-bound evidence checks; it does not by itself prove full span fidelity, source credibility, or truth.** A single lexically-relevant page with a real matching substring can ship as a grounded finding today. Credibility-weighting-in-the-verdict and basket faithfulness are roadmap (§6.2, §9). What the faithfulness engine *enforces* today is literal-span provenance plus verifier checks that **reduce** fabrication risk — a claim is bound to a literal fetched span — and source-quality weighting is the partnership extension; given the disclosed union, bare-count, and judge-injection gaps (§2.1, §9), the engine reduces rather than eliminates the chance a fabricated assertion survives. Demo acceptance requires launching via `run_gate_b.py` (not bare `run_honest_sweep_r3.py`) so `semantic_v2` is live; §9 documents the default-lexical-scorer fact. Closing the source-quality gap (credibility weight in the verdict + basket-as-unit) is part of what the partnership funds.
.\docs\pitch\architecture_appendix_illustrated.md:434:The present-tense answer: day-1 Apodix provides **claim-local, span-bound `strict_verify`** (a generated finding is bound to a literal fetched source span or it does not ship as asserted), unsupported text is **redacted or explicitly labelled a non-finding** rather than asserted, and asserted findings ship with an **offline-re-verifiable signed audit bundle.** For high-stakes clinical/regulatory outputs, that is concrete value: a tamper-evident, third-party-replayable provenance trail for every asserted claim, verifiable with only `gpg` + `sha256sum`, that a frontier model + Fuel iX does not produce. We do not claim a measured completeness advantage over a frontier model; the day-1 edge is **traceability** — asserted findings tied to source spans and packaged in a third-party-replayable audit bundle. The roadmap layer is **basket faithfulness**: corroborating sources promoted into the verdict unit with credibility weight (§6.2, §9). Day-1 value is the shipped single-span provenance core plus signed auditability; the partnership extends that into multi-source verdicts. Apodix is a faithfulness-and-provenance complement to Fuel iX, not a competing assistant — and the live demo is what converts the faithfulness architecture into demonstrated fact.
.\docs\telus\polaris_executive_brief_spine.md:23:**2. How it works (5 steps, plain)** — (a) cast a wide net and *weight* every source by credibility (don't silently drop any); (b) fetch the real full text (incl. paywalled/open-access); (c) draft with a provenance tag on every sentence; (d) verify each sentence against its cited source three independent ways (span-match → entailment → a 4-model adjudication panel); (e) ship a signed, replayable bundle. All on open-weight models — nothing leaves to OpenAI/Google.

.\web\public\canonical_bundles\v1_canonical_success\evidence_pool.json:55:      "full_text": "Treatment with tirzepatide led to significant weight loss in adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities (excluding type 2 diabetes), according to results from the phase 3 SURMOUNT-3 and SURMOUNT-4 studies. Tirzepatide is an investigational once-weekly, dual glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide and glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist that integrates the actions of both incretins into a single molecule. The multicenter, randomized, double-blind, parallel, placebo-controlled SURMOUNT-3 trial (ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: [NCT04657016](https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04657016?term=NCT04657016&rank=1)) evaluated the efficacy and safety of tirzepatide for 72 weeks after a 12-week intensive lifestyle intervention lead-in period in adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related comorbidities, excluding type 2 diabetes. At the end of the 12-week period, study participants (N=579) achieved 6.9% mean weight loss and were randomly assigned 1:1 to receive tirzepatide or placebo subcutaneously once weekly. The efficacy estimand (efficacy prior to discontinuation of study drug) results demonstrated that treatment with tirzepatide was associated with an additional 21.1% reduction in body weight over 72 weeks vs a 3.3% weight regain for the placebo group.
